text: Last class we followed your real workday, from your commute to the wrap-up.
Today we follow the coffee itself, from the farm where it is grown to the cup.

Good morning, Walyson. The buyer from Germany wants to know how this coffee is produced. Can you walk her through the supply chain?
Of course. It all starts at the origin: the coffee is grown on small farms, and the ripe cherries are harvested by hand.
And after the harvest? She is very interested in how the quality is controlled.
After the harvest, the beans are milled and dried, then they are graded by quality before they are exported from Santos.
Perfect. She also asked if a single bag can be traced back to one farm.
Yes. Every lot is traced from the grower to the port, so each blend is documented before it is roasted abroad.
The coffee is grown on small mountain farms.
The ripe cherries are harvested by hand.
The beans are milled, dried, and graded by quality.
The best lots are exported from the port of Santos.
Every lot is traced back to a single grower.

Hi, this is Walyson again. Today I want to walk you through the journey of our coffee, from origin to cup. First, the coffee is grown on small farms in the mountains, where the climate is cool and the altitude is high. When the cherries are ripe, they are harvested by hand, usually between May and September. After the harvest, the cherries are taken to a mill, where the fruit is removed and the green beans are dried in the sun. Then the beans are graded by size and quality, and the best lots are selected for export. From the warehouse in Santos, the coffee is shipped to roasters in Europe and Asia. At the destination, the green beans are roasted, ground, and finally brewed into the cup you enjoy every morning. Every step is recorded, so the coffee can be traced back to a single grower. That is the supply chain, and I am proud to be part of it.

Hi Walyson, this is Carla with an update on the new lot from Minas Gerais. The cherries are picked at this time of year, and the green beans are now graded at the mill. The samples are sent to our lab, and the quality is excellent. This origin is known for a sweet, balanced cup, so it is usually blended with our Santos lots for the European market. The first batch is roasted next Monday, and a small sample is brewed here for the buyers on Tuesday. Everything is traced from the farm to the port, so the client can see exactly where the coffee is grown. The full report is prepared today and sent to you by the evening. Talk soon.

First, the coffee is grown on mountain farms and the ripe cherries are harvested by hand. Next, the cherries are taken to the mill, where the beans are dried and graded by quality. Then the best lots are exported from the port of Santos to roasters abroad. After that, the green beans are roasted and ground at their destination. Finally, the coffee is brewed into the cup, and every step is traced back to the grower.
